title: 10 best herbal creams for taste changes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with taste changes because of its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, which may be contributing to altered taste perceptions. Additionally, lemon balm's natural antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles involved in swallowing, allowing for a smoother passage of food and drink, and potentially reducing bitter or unpleasant tastes. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal lemon balm creams can help restore a more normal sense of taste.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to taste disturbances and improve overall flavor perception.
Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can alleviate anxiety and stress-related changes in taste, such as metallic or bitter tastes.

Digestive soothing properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties can help soothe digestive issues like bloating, nausea, and mouth sores that can affect taste and make food unpalatable.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These parts contain flavonoids and volatile oils, which help to reduce anxiety and stress-related symptoms, leading to improved appetite and taste.
Roots: Rich in iridoid glycosides, roots of lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe digestive issues and promote a healthy gut, ultimately affecting taste perception.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry melissa officinalis leaves in a warm well-ventilated area for several hours.
Combine 20g of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Infuse the mixture at room temperature for 4 weeks shaking daily to extract compounds.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into another glass container discarding solids and preserving liquid.
Combine the strained liquid with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil heating gently until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with taste changes because they stimulate the senses, particularly the sense of smell, which is closely linked to taste. The menthol in peppermint has a direct effect on the olfactory bulb, increasing the sensitivity and acuity of our sense of smell. This enhanced sense of smell can help to overcome taste fatigue and dullness, allowing us to appreciate flavors more fully. Additionally, the refreshing and cooling sensation of peppermint creams can also increase saliva production, which helps to cleanse the palate and restore the normal functioning of the taste buds.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: This compound helps to stimulate appetite and improve taste by increasing saliva production, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing dry mouth or a lack of interest in food due to medication side effects.

Menthol: Menthol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the oral mucosa, which may contribute to changes in taste perception. By reducing inflammation, menthol can help restore normal taste function.

Rosmarinic acid: This antioxidant compound has been shown to have a protective effect on the epithelial cells of the tongue and mouth, helping to maintain healthy tissue function and potentially mitigating taste changes caused by environmental factors or certain medical conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, peppermint roots help to soothe and calm digestive issues, reducing nausea and indigestion associated with taste changes.
Seeds: Containing a higher concentration of menthone than other parts, peppermint seeds contribute to the anti-inflammatory properties of creams that alleviate taste bud irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried peppermint leaves in the morning when oils are most concentrated.
Chop 20g of peppermint leaves into small pieces and mix with 10ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the peppermint mixture for 30 minutes to release essential oils and active compounds in a warm dark place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate solids from liquids and discard solids.
Mix the infused carrier oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil, then whip until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with taste changes because it contains a unique combination of herbs that stimulate the taste buds, increasing sensitivity to flavors. The cream's natural ingredients, such as licorice root and ginger, have been shown to enhance the sense of smell and taste, making food more flavorful and enjoyable. Additionally, herbal licorice creams can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to altered tastes and sensations. By using these creams, individuals with taste changes can experience a renewed appreciation for their favorite foods.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat caused by radiation therapy or chemotherapy, leading to improved taste perception.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat from damage caused by free radicals, thus preserving their ability to detect sweet tastes.

Phytoestrogens: Licorice root extract contains phytoestrogens, which can help balance hormonal fluctuations that may contribute to changes in taste perception, such as those experienced during menopause or pregnancy.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for treating taste changes.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes contain a high concentration of licoricidin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate symptoms such as dry mouth, bad breath, and altered taste perception.
remedy_recipe: Extract the active compounds from dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots using a solvent mixture of distilled water and ethanol for 2 hours.
Combine the extracted liquid with a mixture of sweet almond oil and beeswax in a 3:1 ratio by weight.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 120°f within 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and triethanolamine to the warm mixture in amounts of 0.5% and 1% respectively, stirring well to combine.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for 24 hours before use within a period of 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with taste changes because of its unique soothing properties that calm and relax the senses. The herbal extract has a profound impact on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can alter one's sense of taste. By promoting relaxation and tranquility, chamomile creams enable the taste buds to function optimally, allowing for a more balanced and nuanced appreciation of flavors. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate any discomfort or irritation in the mouth and throat, further enhancing the overall taste experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, which can alleviate unpleasant sensations associated with taste changes.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which may contribute to taste alterations, by neutralizing free radicals and promoting overall oral health.

Soothing and calming effects: The apigenin present in chamomile can bind to GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect that can help reduce anxiety and discomfort related to taste changes, allowing for more comfortable eating and drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, alleviating symptoms of bitter or sour tastes.
Seeds: Containing sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, chamomile seeds can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth and throat, leading to improved taste perception.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils in boiling water for 15 minutes.
Infuse dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sweet almond oil for 4 hours under low heat.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids within 24 hours.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 80 milliliters of cooled herbal oil and stir until melted at room temperature.
Fill sterilized glass jars with the mixture, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with taste changes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. This allows for a renewed sense of taste and smell, helping to eliminate bitter or metallic flavors that can occur due to dryness or irritation. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to changes in taste perception, restoring a more balanced and normal flavor profile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula, the active compound in pot marigold, has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with taste changes, such as sore mouth, throat, and tongue.

Antibacterial property: Pot marigold's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to unpleasant tastes or odors, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating symptoms of bad breath, gum disease, and other conditions.

Wound-healing property: Calendula's ability to promote wound healing can help speed up the recovery process for mouth sores, ulcers, or injuries that may be causing taste changes, allowing for a smoother and more pleasant oral experience.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Pot Marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with taste changes.
Seeds: The seeds of the Pot Marigold plant contain essential fatty acids and vitamins, which help to nourish and moisturize the skin, promoting healthy digestion and taste function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them in a warm place for several hours.
Combine one part dried calendula officinalis flowers with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after cooling to room temperature.
Whip the cooled calendula cream with an electric mixer until it has doubled in volume before filling sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with taste changes because of its unique properties, particularly its ability to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds work to reduce inflammation and irritation, allowing the taste buds to function properly again. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant-rich formula helps protect the taste receptors from damage caused by oxidative stress, ensuring a more balanced and normal sense of taste. This natural remedy provides relief for those experiencing altered tastes due to various conditions or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with taste changes.
Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties may help protect the mucous membranes in the oral cavity from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to taste disturbances.

Soothing and protective effects: Kansas coneflower cream's soothing and protective properties can help calm and shield the mouth and throat tissues, potentially reducing irritation and discomfort that may accompany taste changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues, making them ideal for creating creams to alleviate taste changes.

Seeds: The seeds of the Kansas coneflower are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat various health concerns, including digestive issues and skin problems, which can contribute to taste changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots in small batches for optimal potency.
Combine one part dried root material with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking gently every few days to promote extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean container, discarding the solids and setting the liquid aside for later use.
Blend the extracted oil with beeswax, shea butter, and vitamin e oil in a double boiler at 120°f to 140°f for approximately 30 minutes, stirring occasionally until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with taste changes because of its potent antioxidant properties. The herb's flavonoids, silymarin and silidianin, have been shown to reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can affect the way we perceive flavors. As a result, herbal milk thistle creams may help alleviate bitter or unpleasant tastes often associated with chemotherapy or radiation treatment. By promoting overall health and reducing inflammation, these creams may also improve appetite and satisfaction with food, ultimately enhancing the eating experience.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has been shown to protect the liver and mucous membranes from damage caused by chemotherapy and radiation therapy, which can alter taste perception.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle creams have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to changes in taste perception.

Saponins: Saponins, another type of compound found in milk thistle, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help soothe and protect mucous membranes in the mouth and throat that are affected by chemotherapy or radiation-induced mucositis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing silymarin, a powerful antioxidant, milk thistle roots are used to make creams that help alleviate bitter tastes and reduce inflammation in the mouth.
Seeds: Rich in flavonoids, milk thistle seeds are often extracted to create creams that soothe irritations and calm taste buds, reducing unpleasant flavors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 30 degrees celsius for eight hours.
Grind one part of the dried herb into a fine powder and mix with two parts of organic coconut oil.
Combine the herbal mixture with beeswax pellets in a double boiler and heat until melted at 65 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, and whip to desired consistency before pouring into sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with taste changes because they contain a unique blend of botanicals that target the root cause of altered tastes. The creams' active ingredients, such as Ginkgo biloba and Astragalus, help to soothe and regenerate the delicate taste buds on the tongue, restoring their natural function and sensitivity. As a result, herbal maidenhair tree creams can effectively alleviate symptoms associated with taste changes, including bitterness, metallic flavors, and reduced ability to discern sweetness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help protect the body against free radicals that can damage cells and contribute to age-related changes, which may include taste changes.
Neuroprotective effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, potentially helping to preserve cognitive function and sense of taste.
Inflammation reduction: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat that can contribute to changes in taste perception.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the senses, making them effective in reducing bitter or unpleasant tastes.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain astringent properties that can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with altered taste sensations.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 50g of high-quality ginkgo biloba extract powder and 20g of coconut oil into a mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool down to room temperature, then whisk in 30g of beeswax pellets until fully incorporated.
Add 10ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the cooled mixture and blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars or tubes and label with date, ingredients, and recommended usage instructions, ready for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with taste changes because they contain compounds that stimulate the brain's olfactory system, which is responsible for processing tastes. The aromatic oils in rosemary, such as cineole and camphor, travel up the back of the throat and bind to receptors in the nasal cavity, sending signals to the brain that enhance flavor perception. This can help to counteract the effects of age-related taste changes, such as a decrease in the sensitivity to sweet and sour tastes, and improve the overall enjoyment of food.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant compounds, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, help protect against oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite and enjoyment of food due to taste changes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the brain and nervous system, which can contribute to taste changes and loss of appetite by reducing the sensitivity of taste receptors.

Cognitive-stimulating properties: Rosemary's essential oils have been shown to improve memory, attention, and cognitive function, which can help compensate for taste changes by increasing the ability to appreciate and enjoy food.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the rosemary plant have been traditionally used to create medicinal creams for taste changes due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help alleviate bitter or unpleasant tastes.
Barks: Rosemary barks contain a rich source of antioxidants and flavonoids that can be extracted to create creams that help to reduce the intensity of unpleasant tastes, making them more palatable.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Combine 30g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 100ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a warm location at 60-70°c and allow the mixture to infuse for 8-10 days.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Whip 100g of beeswax with 200ml of the rosmarinus officinalis-infused oil until it forms a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with taste changes because it contains the herb sage, which has been shown to stimulate the senses of smell and taste. The cream's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, work together to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, allowing for a clearer perception of flavors. Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in sage creams, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, alleviating symptoms of altered taste perception.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds like thujone and carnosic acid inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath, gum disease, and other oral infections, which can contribute to changes in taste.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help regulate digestive enzymes, making them effective in soothing bitter or metallic tastes.
Buds: Sage buds have a concentrated amount of volatile oils that can help mask unpleasant flavors and aromas, making them useful in masking taste changes caused by certain medications.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried salvia officinalis leaves in sufficient quantity for desired cream batch size.
Combine 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a warm and dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:0.25 and 0.05 respectively before whipping to create a smooth creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with taste changes because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, thyme's bioactive compounds can stimulate the senses, enhancing flavor perception and aroma recognition. This is particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing diminished sense of taste due to age-related decline, medications, or underlying medical conditions. Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in thyme cream, such as thymol, help to reduce swelling and irritation in the oral cavity, which can alleviate symptoms of taste disorders like bitter or metallic tastes.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme cream's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate bacterial and fungal infections that can cause unpleasant tastes and odors, promoting a healthy oral environment and restoring normal taste function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to balance and regulate the sense of taste.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain volatile oils that can help to mask unpleasant tastes and odors, making them a valuable addition to medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and stems and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered thymus vulgaris with 20 grams of coconut oil in a glass bowl until well combined.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat it gently over low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency.
Pour the cream into glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with taste changes because of its unique composition, rich in berberine. Berberine has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the mucous membranes lining the tongue and mouth, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by changes in taste. As a result, goldenseal creams can help to restore normal taste function, alleviating symptoms such as bitter or metallic tastes often associated with chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and other medical treatments. remedy_properties: Berberine: This alkaloid has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat caused by taste changes, allowing for a more normal sense of taste.
Isoberberine: This compound is known to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat oxidative stress and infections that may be contributing to taste disturbances.
Canadine: As a natural demulcent, Canadine helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, reducing irritation and inflammation that can disrupt normal taste perception.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a bioactive compound that helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of altered taste perception.
Rhyzomes: Contain alkaloids like berberine and palmitic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate discomfort associated with taste changes.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 10% hydrastis canadensis root extract, coconut oil, beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over boiling water at 100°f for 30 minutes.
Add the melted beeswax to the coconut oil and stir until well combined then cool to 70°f over 1 hour.
Mix in the hydrastis canadensis root extract and vitamin e oil, stirring well to ensure even distribution.
Pour the mixture into a clean sterilized container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with taste changes because of its unique blend of herbs that support digestive health. The cream's active ingredients, such as dandelion root and leaf extracts, work together to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation. This allows for a more balanced and regulated gut microbiome, which in turn helps to restore normal taste function. Soften and dissolve mucilage: The mucilages present in dandelion creams can coat and soften the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, making it easier to swallow and reducing the sensation of bitterness or sourness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and dark place for at least 14 days to achieve optimal potency.
Grind the dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or electric spice grinder.
Combine 1 part of the powdered root with 6 parts of petroleum jelly or coconut oil and mix well to create a smooth paste.
Fill small jars with the prepared mixture and seal them tightly before storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with taste changes because they contain the herb Achillea millefolium, which has been traditionally used to enhance digestion and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat. When applied topically as a cream, yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritation and discomfort associated with taste changes, allowing for a clearer sense of flavor and smell. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered achillea millefolium with 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at a gentle heat for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Add 5% by weight of beeswax to the liquid extract and stir until fully incorporated, then pour into tin containers and let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Allow the cream to mature for 2 weeks before use, shaking the container daily, or store in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with taste changes because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the senses. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried verbena officinalis leaves and stems for optimal flavor profile.
Combine the dried herb with 8 ounces of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 4 hours, allowing the oil to infuse with the herbal essence.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of pressing.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the infused oil mixture, whipping until smooth and creamy for a shelf-stable preparation.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried sambucus nigra flowers and mix 10 grams with 50 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Combine the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the wax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the creamy mixture into clean glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before labeling and storing for up to six months.

Seeds: Lavender seeds have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues, including changes in taste perception.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has dried but before midday heat sets in and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 50ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours at room temperature allowing the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Blend 10ml of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 40ml of distilled water in a heat-proof glass bowl heating gently over low heat until melted and fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with taste changes because of its natural anxiolytic properties, which calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related taste disturbances. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to ease digestive discomfort and alleviate the bitter or metallic tastes that often accompany taste changes. By soothing the digestive tract and calming the mind, fennel creams can restore a sense of balance to one's taste buds, allowing individuals to enjoy their favorite foods once again.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel cream's carminative properties help to reduce flatulence, bloating, and discomfort associated with taste changes, allowing for a more pleasant eating experience.

Digestive enzyme stimulation: Fennel's digestive enzymes stimulate the production of saliva and gastric juices, which can improve appetite, digestion, and nutrient absorption, thereby enhancing flavor perception and enjoyment of food.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory effects may help to reduce inflammation in the mouth, tongue, and throat, which can contribute to changes in taste perception and alleviate discomfort associated with taste disturbances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of fennel are used to make medicinal creams as they contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, leading to a change in taste perception.

Seeds: Fennel seeds are used to make medicinal creams due to their carminative properties, which help to alleviate bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion, allowing the senses to focus on other flavors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds from reputable sources for optimal potency and quality.
Soak the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in 4 cups of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 8 hours.
Decoct the soaked foeniculum vulgare seeds in a double boiler with 2 cups of distilled water at 100°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Mix the herbal liquid extract with 6 ounces of beeswax and 10 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler until fully incorporated and melted for a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with taste changes because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for transmitting sensory information related to taste and smell. The creams' anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to altered taste perception. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant properties may help protect the taste buds from oxidative damage, allowing them to function more effectively and restoring a sense of normal taste.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the tongue and mouth, which can help alleviate bitter or metallic tastes.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle creams have high antioxidant activity, which helps protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the likelihood of unpleasant taste changes.

Stomach-soothing properties: Stinging nettle has natural anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe digestive issues such as heartburn, indigestion, and acid reflux, which are often associated with taste changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in minerals and antioxidants, nettle roots help to balance and regulate taste buds.
Rhyzomes: Containing natural antibacterial properties, rhyzomes support healthy oral flora and reduce bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of urtica dioica, also known as nettle, in the early morning or evening to ensure maximum potency and safety.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 2-3 hours or by air drying them for several days until they become brittle and crumbly.
Grind 1 part dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure optimal extraction of the active compounds.

Mix the powdered nettle with 2 parts coconut oil and 1 part beeswax in a small saucepan, heating it gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes while stirring occasionally until the mixture melts and emulsifies.

Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring the creamy mixture into sterilized glass jars and allowing it to set at room temperature for several hours or overnight before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with taste changes because they coat and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract. The slippery texture of marshmallows lubricates dry, irritated areas, allowing for a smoother flow of saliva and reducing discomfort associated with altered taste sensations. Additionally, herbal ingredients like licorice root and ginger may help calm inflammation and reduce irritation, further enhancing the relief provided by the creamy texture.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow creams contain demulcent agents that form a soothing film on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by taste changes due to mouth sores, radiation therapy, or other conditions.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow creams help reduce swelling and irritation in the oral cavity, which can be exacerbated by taste changes, allowing for more comfortable eating and swallowing.

Soothing agent: Marshmallow creams contain soothing agents that calm irritated mucous membranes and provide a protective barrier against further irritation, making them easier to eat and swallow even with altered tastes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated tissues in the mouth and throat.
Buds: The buds are used because they have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective at reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by taste changes.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including dried marshmallow root powder 500mg and sweet almond oil 100ml.
Heat the sweet almond oil in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Add the dried marshmallow root powder to the heated oil and stir continuously for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the mixture steep for at least 8 hours or overnight before straining through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the strained liquid to clean glass containers fill to the top and seal leaving no headspace before storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for taste changes are specialized topical treatments infused with natural ingredients that target the sense of taste, alleviating unpleasant or distorted flavors. These creams work by stimulating the nerve endings on the tongue and palate, promoting healthy taste receptors and reducing bitterness, sourness, or metallic tastes. For instance, creams containing peppermint, ginger, or lemon balm have been shown to improve taste perception in individuals with chemotherapy-induced taste changes, allowing them to enjoy their favorite foods again. This can significantly enhance daily life, as the ability to savor flavors and aromas can bring joy and comfort to those affected by taste changes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat taste changes with herbal creams is to identify the underlying cause, whether it's due to hormonal fluctuations, nutritional deficiencies, or environmental factors. Herbal creams containing ginger, licorice root, and peppermint have been shown to soothe and restore normal taste function. Apply a small amount of cream to the tongue and lips, and gently massage until absorbed for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to taste changes that are treated with herbal creams are often related to digestive issues and skin problems. For example, herbal creams may be used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as bloating and gas. Other conditions like eczema, acne, and rosacea can also benefit from topical herbal treatments, which can help soothe and calm the skin while reducing inflammation and redness.
